ЁЯЪАSpring Cloud: Eureka service discovery тАФ call services by name, not IP.

тЬЕ Each service registers with Eureka on startup (spring.application.name)

тЬЕ Eureka tracks healthy instances via heartbeats

тЬЕ Clients call http://author-service/... тАФ LoadBalancer resolves the name to a live instance

ЁЯЪАSpring Cloud: OpenFeign + Circuit Breaker тАФ resilience isn't an afterthought.

тЬЕ @FeignClient calls remote services by name (via Eureka + LoadBalancer)

тЬЕ @CircuitBreaker stops calling a failing service after a threshold

тЬЕ fallbackMethod returns a safe default while the circuit is OPEN

ЁЯЪАSpring Cloud: Contract testing with Pact тАФ make sure two services still agree on their API.

тЬЕ The consumer writes down the response it expects and tests against a mock тАФ the real provider doesn't need to be running

тЬЕ The provider replays that same contract against the real app and fails if it no longer matches

тЬЕ Each side is verified separately, so a breaking change shows up long before deployment

ЁЯЪАSpring Cloud: Load testing with Gatling тАФ check a service survives real traffic.

тЬЕ Describe a scenario (e.g. POST /library/book) and ramp virtual users up over time

тЬЕ Feed randomized data with Faker so every request looks realistic

тЬЕ Assert SLAs on the whole run тАФ fail it if it gets too slow (тЙд10s) or drops below >90% success

ЁЯТб Java 17 тАФ sealed types close the inheritance graph with `permits`.

тЬЕ Only listed subtypes may extend the sealed class/interface

тЬЕ Each permitted subtype must be `final`, `sealed`, or `non-sealed`

тЬЕ `non-sealed` is the escape hatch тАФ inheritance opens again below that branch

ЁЯТб Java 14+ switch expressions тАФ `switch` returns a value, and the compiler makes sure you didn't forget a case.

тЬЕ Old switch: forget `break` and execution falls through to the next case

тЬЕ New `case X -> "..."`: one line per case, returns a value, no `break` needed

тЬЕ Add a new enum constant without a matching case? Compile error, not a silent bug at runtime

ЁЯТб Share data between threads without `volatile` on every field.

тЬЕ Thread A writes data, thread B reads a flag тАФ B might see the flag change but still read stale data

тЬЕ `volatile` forces every read and write to sync тАФ safe, but heavier than you often need

тЬЕ `setRelease` (publish) + `getAcquire` (subscribe): writer flips the flag when done; reader then sees all prior writes

enum DataScope {
    CUSTOMERS_READ,
    CUSTOMERS_WRITE,
    CUSTOMERS_UPDATE,
    CUSTOMERS_DELETE,
    ORDERS_READ,
    ORDERS_WRITE,
    ORDERS_UPDATE,
    ORDERS_DELETE,
    PRODUCTS_READ,
    PRODUCTS_WRITE,
    PRODUCTS_UPDATE,
    PRODUCTS_DELETE,
    INVENTORY_READ,
    INVENTORY_WRITE,
    INVENTORY_UPDATE,
    INVENTORY_DELETE,
    PAYMENTS_READ,
    PAYMENTS_WRITE,
    PAYMENTS_UPDATE,
    PAYMENTS_DELETE,
    SHIPMENTS_READ,
    SHIPMENTS_WRITE,
    SHIPMENTS_UPDATE,
    SHIPMENTS_DELETE,
    INVOICES_READ,
    INVOICES_WRITE,
    INVOICES_UPDATE,
    INVOICES_DELETE,
    REPORTS_READ,
    REPORTS_WRITE,
    REPORTS_UPDATE,
    REPORTS_DELETE,
    USERS_READ,
    USERS_WRITE,
    USERS_UPDATE,
    USERS_DELETE,
    ROLES_READ,
    ROLES_WRITE,
    ROLES_UPDATE,
    ROLES_DELETE,
    AUDIT_READ,
    AUDIT_WRITE,
    AUDIT_UPDATE,
    AUDIT_DELETE,
    CONFIG_READ,
    CONFIG_WRITE,
    CONFIG_UPDATE,
    CONFIG_DELETE,
}

// 48 flags тАФ EnumSet: bit vector (~48 bits)
// HashSet: 48 entries + buckets + boxing overhead

// Build the full set
EnumSet<DataScope> admin = EnumSet.allOf(DataScope.class);
Set<DataScope> adminHash = new HashSet<>(Arrays.asList(DataScope.values()));

// Bulk change тАФ bitwise OR/AND on a few machine words
EnumSet<DataScope> ordersDomain = EnumSet.of(
    DataScope.ORDERS_READ, DataScope.ORDERS_WRITE,
    DataScope.ORDERS_UPDATE, DataScope.ORDERS_DELETE);
admin.removeAll(ordersDomain);

EnumSet<DataScope> readOnly = EnumSet.copyOf(admin);
readOnly.removeIf(p -> !p.name().endsWith("_READ"));

// contains() тАФ O(1) bit test, not hash + bucket walk
boolean ok = admin.contains(DataScope.PAYMENTS_READ);
